publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-22 00:09:03 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Papers collected by Alan K. McAdams about the U.S. vs. IBM anti-trust case include a reasonably complete transcript of the trial; an additional copy of the transcript of McAdams' own testimony; exhibits used with McAdams' testimony; working papers and drafts used for the exhibits; depositions of other economists who appeared; other documents; and some personal files of Alan K. McAdams.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Alan K. McAdams is a professor of managerial economics at the Cornell Graduate School of Management, and was the chief economic witness for the government in the IBM anti-trust case.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Papers collected by Alan K. McAdams about the U.S. vs. IBM anti-trust case include a reasonably complete transcript of the trial; an additional copy of the transcript of McAdams' own testimony; exhibits used with McAdams' testimony; working papers and drafts used for the exhibits; depositions of other economists who appeared; other documents; and some personal files of Alan K. McAdams.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Alan K. McAdams. Papers, #4068. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: United States of America vs. International Business Machines Corporation.
Monopolies.
Competition.
Antitrust law.
Computers -- Law and legislation.
Computer industry -- United States.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: A. D. Little technical info (mainly Withington) with some material on telecommunications
Acquisition of Quantor Corp. by NCR
Addendum to opinion of defendant's objections to prelim. Report
Amended answers to IBM requests for admissions (set II)
Appraisal of U.S. vs. I.B.M. pp. 1-306
Appraisal of U.S. bs. IBM parts II & III
Back up materials to verify gov't charts
Brief for U.S. on rehearsing En Banc
Brilogg Depo Notes Capital Budgeting Decision
Cary digest, MRX, US, TELEX vs. IBM
Clearer copies of docs in evidence
Cogley on Naval Academy GE installation
Computer systems organized for commercial user
Comstat charts & copy of blue-penciled charts
Docs used to derive companies other than IBM data
Documents on tapes, disks, & internal info. Systems
Exxon strategic guidelines for technical planning study
Historical Narrative Statement of Mancke, Fisher and McKie
IBM amended ans. to IBM requests for admission (set IV)
IBM Lease Base Statistics Users Guide
IBM stipulation of fact, model 90 program
IBM witness list in IBM vs. MRX
IBM's conduct in the display terminal market
IBM's lack of foresights, skill & industry
Joint glossary of EDP terms - part II
Letters from U.S. General Accounting Office
McAdams findings - Eastman, Baenziger, Laudati (2 folders)
Memo in opposition to precluding McPherson testimony
Memo on market defn. for antitrust purposes
Misc. docs, transcripts, reports, data (3 mail parcels)
Non-party docs not now in system
Plaintiff's Statement of Assumed Peripheral Market Definition Facts
Procedures to date gov't est. charts
Report to the Congress (2 with illegible dates)
Response to IBM Motion for Mistrial
Response to IBM's request for admissions
Schedule of docs. demanded by IBM
Smith Kline vs. Eli Lilly - appeal
Spare Prolevsky findings of fact - proposed
The computer industry - the next 5 years
U.S. Dept. of Agr. disk drives
